danger_signals.visual_form: Obstacles and hostile creatures positioned throughout level environments
danger_signals.meaning: Areas where player must engage combat or avoid contact to prevent damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Star-based health icon system in upper-right corner; stars disappear when damaged
danger_signals.meaning: Visual feedback showing proximity to losing a life; four-star maximum capacity

danger_signals.visual_form: Level architecture with dangerous obstacles requiring navigation or manipulation
danger_signals.meaning: Physical threats that can be countered through arm-extension mechanics

danger_signals.visual_form: Brainwashed planet leaders serving Kaiser Greedy (narrative element affecting world state)
danger_signals.meaning: Corrupted authority figures representing altered political control

safety_signals.visual_form: Star figures placed throughout levels; yellow stars restore one health unit, blue stars restore full health
Ristar figure items hidden within levels
safety_signals.meaning: Extended survival capacity and continue allowance

safety_signals.visual_form: Reaching level end via specialized Star Handle launch mechanism
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage to next planetary stage; bonus points awarded based on altitude achieved

safety_signals.visual_form: Hidden Star Handles placed within level design
safety_signals.meaning: Optional challenge areas for skill demonstration and continue earning

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mind-control authority; space pirate faction identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Brainwashed planet leaders under Greedy's control; underlings Inonis and Uranim
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corrupted planetary leadership; enforced obedience across multiple worlds in Valdi System

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shooting star entity; celestial hero archetype
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Liberating enslaved planets; restoring free will to brainwashed leaders

faction_indicators.faction: Oruto (Japanese version) / Legendary Hero Father (International version)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Source of Ristar's mission; represents benevolent cosmic power opposing tyranny

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each planet (Flora/Neer, etc.) presents corrupted leadership and obedient citizens; environmental design reflects oppression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Brainwashed leaders serve Greedy; inhabitants pray for salvation (Japanese version) or remain subjugated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential level progression through Valdi System planets; each completed stage represents restored planetary freedom
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defeated leaders and neutralized mind-control influence; Greedy's power diminishes through game progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final ending: Japanese version shows Greedy and underlings stranded on deserted planet with Ristar's image appearing in space; International version shows father reunion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tyranny overthrown; cosmic balance restored; protagonist achieves objective (rescue or liberation)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health system displayed as four-star maximum; scattered restorative items require active collection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate carefully to locate health/life pickups; resource management affects survival strategy

environmental_storytelling.example: Each world in Valdi System presents distinct visual identity; Planet Neer/Flora establishes setting as biological/natural world requiring hero intervention
Brainwashed planet leaders visible through level design and narrative; their control indicates scope of Greedy's tyranny
Ristar presented as shooting star entity summoned/awakened by benevolent cosmic force; visual archetype establishes moral alignment
Hidden Star Handles and bonus stages suggest exploration rewards; Ristar figures scattered throughout indicate hero's significance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Uses extendable arms to attack enemies via grab-and-headbutt motion; manipulates environment through arm extension; swings on poles and Star Handles to traverse levels
Sparkles appear behind character; becomes invincible and defeats any enemy on contact; sustained by momentum from Star Handle spinning; flight ceases after momentum loss (few seconds)
Can be grabbed via extended arms and defeated through headbutt collision; some enemies serve as swingable platforms mid-air
Obey Kaiser Greedy; represent corrupted authority; implied to resist or flee after Greedy's defeat

notes: Ristar communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing (Greedy's galactic tyranny) and level progression structure (sequential planetary liberation). Visual manifestation relies on color-coded health system and environmental design reflecting planetary corruption. The game lacks complex faction sigils or color-coded enemy types; instead, it uses story context and character archetype (cosmic hero vs. space pirate tyrant) to establish moral and political opposition. International localization shifted paternal/maternal origin story, altering motivation framing without changing core danger/safety signal system.
